Deploy SGSC-AI protocol to analyze the supply chain risk and uncover the true industry role of Nanjing Xunzheng Electronic Technology Co., Ltd..

**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Triggering the advanced military/aerospace cover stripping mechanism and the industrial park 'stall-ification' demotion probe. This enterprise's business license is akin to a 'military-industrial tycoon' in the lower-tier market: from 'radar equipment design, manufacturing' to 'aerospace equipment and parts processing,' and further to 'railway vehicle accessories,' its scope spans the absolute top-tier precision manufacturing fields across land, sea, and air. Naturally, the commercial system judged it to be in the 'Electronic Device Manufacturing' broad category. However, the forensic logic engine showed no mercy facing such a 'cyber military factory,' directly cutting into its physical coordinates: 'No. A-085,' 219 Lantian Road, Lukou Sub-district, Jiangning District. This type of address with a letter and a three-digit number (A-085) is the typical numbering format for internal stalls/small cubicles in large hardware and electromechanical cities, auto parts cities, or low-end mechanical processing markets. Simultaneously, the demographic probe captured: an insured headcount of 17 people and a paid-in capital of 1.9 million RMB. The logic reconstruction is extremely clear: 17 people with less than 2 million in funds squeezed into a stall or small workshop in an electromechanical market to manufacture military radar and aerospace equipment? This constitutes a highly absurd industrial rhapsody. Piercing through all the 'aerospace' boasting, the probe locked onto the actual, grounded business in the latter half of its license: 'mechanical parts processing, metal cutting and welding, electromechanical equipment maintenance.' The truth finally lands: this is absolutely not an aerospace manufacturing giant, but a Metal Machining & Welding Workshop inside an electromechanical market. These 17 people (including a few lathe operators, millers, and welders) rely on a few machine tools to take on surrounding mechanical parts subcontracting, metal cutting, and equipment repair jobs. As for 'aerospace and radar,' it is highly likely a tag forcefully added by the boss just to be able to catch a few peripheral, marginal parts processing orders.
